chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with recurrent chest pain.
history: The patient had a 5-year history of taking aspirin for coronary artery disease. He recently underwent stent implantation. He complained of fatigue and was found to have megaloblastic anemia and thrombocytopenia.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, RR 20/min. Physical examination revealed no significant abnormalities.
labs: ECG: normal. Blood tests: Hemoglobin 8.5 g/dL, Platelet count 50 x 10^9/L, Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) 110 fL. Coronary angiography revealed severe stenosis in the left anterior descending artery with a TIMI blood flow grade of 2.
correct_diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome with aspirin-related enteropathy
differentials: Acute coronary syndrome with aspirin-related enteropathy
Acute coronary syndrome with NSAID-related enteropathy
Acute coronary syndrome with clopidogrel resistance
Acute coronary syndrome with stent thrombosis
Acute coronary syndrome with unknown etiology
correct_ranking: Acute coronary syndrome with aspirin-related enteropathy
Acute coronary syndrome with NSAID-related enteropathy
Acute coronary syndrome with clopidogrel resistance
Acute coronary syndrome with stent thrombosis
Acute coronary syndrome with unknown etiology
